Eurozone Gross Bond Issuance Expected Higher, Net Supply Stable in 2026 — Market Talk
data.description: Gross government bond issuance in the eurozone is expected to rise to 1.42 trillion euros in 2026 from 1.33 trillion euros in 2025, according to TD Securities' forecasts. About half of that increase is set to occur in Germany, they say in a note. "The uptick is broadly due to refinancing needs however, and we expect net issuance from the 11 largest Euro Area economies to be stable." A decline in Italian net borrowing is set to offset an increase in German needs, which will help peripheral government bond yield spreads over Germany remain compressed, the strategists say. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Bitcoin Coinbase Premium Crashes to -$90, What Does It Mean?
data.description:
Bitcoin has been in the red for four straight weeks. Bitcoin extended its drop from late October, reaching a low of $88,483 this week, to print four red weekly candles. The drop has caused a loss of crucial weekly support, the weekly MA 50 at $102,749.

As a result, the Coinbase Premium Gap dropped as low as -$90, according to CryptoQuant, a sign of strong selling pressure in the U.S.

According to CryptoQuant, selling continues to dominate in the American session. Cumulative trading returns during U.S. hours are negative for the entire month, while Europe and APAC remain flat or slightly positive.

CryptoQuant.com
@cryptoquant_com

Bitcoin has been red for four straight weeks.

The Coinbase Premium Gap dropped as low as -$90, which is a sign of strong U.S. selling pressure.

Here’s the key data behind the U.S. investor sell-off 👇 pic.twitter.com/V9liGZmmcs

Nov 19, 2025

This has resulted in ETF flows turning net negative for three consecutive weeks as outflows continue to weigh on the BTC spot market. Investors pulled more than half a billion dollars from BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust, the largest single-day outflow since the fund’s debut. BlackRock saw $523 million in outflows from the exchange-traded fund on Tuesday, marking the fifth straight day of net outflows and its highest on record.

Bitcoin ETFs are now down $3.98 billion from their all-time high, which is the second-largest drawdown since launch, as the BTC ETF realized price sits at $86,586.

What it means

The Coinbase Bitcoin Premium Index measures the price difference between Bitcoin traded on top U.S. exchange Coinbase and the global market average. It marks a key indicator of capital flows, institutional activity and market sentiment.

A negative premium suggests Bitcoin is trading for less on Coinbase. This might indicate selling pressure on the market and waning risk appetite among investors.

data.title: Walmart sales keep climbing, and it's more than just the wealthy looking for bargains
data.description:
By Tomi Kilgore

Sales at Sam's Club stores open more than a year rose less than Wall Street had expected. When rival Costco last reported, the average person was spending less than a year earlier.

Walmart as a whole beat profit and sales expectations, but the stock fell as the Sam's Club business disappointed.

Shares of Walmart took a hit in early Thursday trading, after the big-box retail behemoth beat Wall Street's forecasts for overall third-quarter profit and sales but saw a disappointing performance at its Sam's Club business.

The company (WMT) said it gained market share in several categories, including grocery, health and wellness and general merchandise, and saw strong growth in membership-based income. And while Walmart raised its outlook for both sales growth and profit, it wasn't enough to please investors.

According to a Numerator analysis in June, Walmart had accounted for about a quarter of SNAP recipients' spending on consumer packaged goods and general merchandise.

That's because the results come at a time of some anxiety for investors. Walmart has certainly been on a roll, with a long streak of sales beats, as the aggressive buildout of its digital strategy has helped the retailer broaden its reach to comprise more than just customers seeking deep discounts. But a surprise change in leadership announced last week may leave investors worried if the run of success can continue.

Don't miss: Who is John Furner, Walmart's new CEO?

The stock lost 1.1% in premarket trading, to put it on track to open at a 21/2-month low, and to suffer a fourth straight one-day, post-earnings decline.

Comparable sales, or sales rung up at stores open more than a year, for the Walmart U.S. business grew 4.5% from the same period a year ago, as the number of transactions was up 1.8% and the value of the average ticket rose 2.7%. That beat the average analyst estimate compiled by FactSet for comparable-sales growth of 4%, to extend the streak of beats to 15 quarters. Comparable sales can be a better gauge of sales performance than net sales, which can increase solely because store locations have opened.

To put Sam's Club's business in perspective, net sales of $23.6 billion represented 20% of Walmart U.S. net sales of $120.7 billion.

But for the Sam's Club U.S. membership-based warehouse business, comparable sales increased 3.8%, missing the FactSet consensus for a 4.9% rise, as transactions were up 3.9% but the average ticket dipped 0.1%. That compares with U.S. growth in rival Costco Wholesale's (COST) latest quarter of 5.1%.

To put Sam's Club's business in perspective, net sales of $23.6 billion represented 20% of Walmart U.S. net sales of $120.7 billion.

Total revenue increased 5.8% to $179.5 billion, above the FactSet consensus of $177.44 billion, as net sales grew 5.8% and membership and other income, which represented just 1% of total revenue, increased 9%.

Net income jumped 34.2% to $6.14 billion, while adjusted earnings per share, which excludes nonrecurring items, of 62 cents topped the FactSet consensus of 60 cents.

Looking ahead, the company lifted its guidance ranges for adjusted EPS to $2.58 to $2.63 from $2.52 to $2.62 and for net sales growth to 4.8% to 5.1% from 3.75% to 4.75%.

Ahead of the results, some analysts had said Walmart could be conservative with its outlook, given uncertainties over how the federal-government shutdown would disrupt Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program benefits, also known as "SNAP," or food stamps. According to a Numerator analysis in June, Walmart had accounted for about a quarter of SNAP recipients' spending on consumer packaged goods and general merchandise.

Walmart's stock has gained 11.4% in 2025 through Wednesday, compared with the 36.3% tumble in rival Target shares (TGT) over the same timeframe. But the stock has pulled back 7.7% since it closed at a record $109.03 on Oct. 15 and has lost 1.9% since its CEO change was announced.

Bill Peters contributed.

Read on: Walmart moving stock-market listing to Nasdaq from NYSE

-Tomi Kilgore

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Walmart Well Positioned in Current Environment With Evolution Toward Tech — Market Talk
data.description: Walmart continues to stand out in a challenging retail environment, according to D.A. Davidson in a research note. The retailer's 3Q results are "continued evidence that Walmart is well positioned in the current environment, offering value for lower-income customers who are feeling the weight of what seems like a slowing consumer environment, as well as convenience for higher-income customers," analysts Michael Baker and Keegan Cox write. Walmart will move its stock listing to Nasdaq next month, a move the analysts say could be a nod to its continued evolution as a tech-powered retailer, poised to win an increasingly online and agentic commerce world.(connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: Reuters Gulf Currents Newsletter – MBS gets F-35s and a US shield
data.description:
By Andrew Mills

Deputy Bureau Chief, Gulf

This week’s Gulf Currents features a special guest analysis by Samia Nakhoul, Reuters’ global foreign policy editor and former Middle East Editor. She dissects U.S. President Donald Trump’s audacious bid to put Riyadh on par with Israel.

Plus: Washington greenlights the export of advanced AI chips to Saudi Arabia and the UAE; Dubai seizes luxury villas bought with suspected French drug money; fresh scrutiny of Saudi Arabia’s trillion-dollar U.S. investment pledge; China’s state-owned COMAC makes a Dubai Airshow fly-by; and a chart on Saudi labor reforms. Dive in.

NEWS BRIEFING

-Washington authorized the export of advanced artificial intelligence chips—the equivalent of 35,000 Nvidia Blackwell chips—to Saudi Arabia’s Humain and the UAE’s G42, two state-backed AI ventures. The announcement, which coincided with MBS’s visit to Washington, is a big show of support by the U.S. for the two countries’ AI ambitions.

-The UAE agreed to seize around 40 Dubai luxury villas and apartments suspected of having been bought using cash or cryptocurrencies linked to drug trafficking in France. Paris had secured the extradition of 14 alleged major traffickers from the UAE so far this year and has requested the arrest and extradition of around 15 more "highly dangerous" individuals.

TRUMP’S BIG BET: SAUDI-ISRAEL PARITY AND A NEW ORDER, by Samia Nakhoul, Reuters Global Foreign Policy Editor

President Donald Trump shattered a decades-old U.S. policy by putting Saudi Arabia on strategic parity with Israel—offering Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, known as MBS, coveted F-35 fighter jets and a defense guarantee at a White House visit.

For decades, Washington preserved Israel’s military edge as sacrosanct. Trump broke that doctrine, unlocking the F-35 monopoly for Riyadh—though the jets will be less advanced than those supplied to Israel—while pressing for his longtime goal: Saudi-Israeli normalisation.

The move rattled Jerusalem and marks the boldest U.S. policy shift in the region in decades.

For MBS, the optics are transformative: from pariah to power broker, now shielded by an American security umbrella and elevated to Israel’s level. The deal cemented Riyadh's regional rise and MBS as Washington’s pivotal partner.

The stakes are enormous: military parity, strategic alliances, and a new Middle East order—if the agreements survive congressional scrutiny.

In an unmatched flurry of White House pomp—featuring a procession of horses and military flyover—Trump offered MBS global validation, hailing his “friend” and reforms that swept away decades of austere social codes.

And slain Saudi critic Jamal Khashoggi? History. Trump brushed aside the 2018 murder at the hands of Saudi operatives linked to MBS, shielding the crown prince from scrutiny—contradicting a U.S. intelligence assessment. MBS denied involvement.

Trump’s calculus: Saudi Arabia as the fulcrum as a linchpin in efforts to counter China and Iran. Whether this gamble delivers or destabilises the region will define his second term legacy—and his ambition to redraw the Middle East map under U.S. patronage.

SAUDI’S TRILLION-DOLLAR PROMISE: BIG TALK, THIN DETAILS

When Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman told Donald Trump this week that Saudi Arabia would boost its investment in the U.S. from $600 billion to $1 trillion, Trump quipped: “Could you make it $1.5 trillion?”

Cue awkward laughter, but no timeline, no specifics.

We’ve been here before. Back in May, Trump’s Gulf tour generated headlines touting $2 trillion in deals. Reuters crunched the numbers then: actual agreements were closer to $700 billion, much of it non-binding MoUs signed against a backdrop of political theatre. Analysts warned that “possible spending is counted as actual” and that many pledges would quietly fade once the photo ops ended.

Fast forward, and Riyadh is juggling the construction of several giga projects at home, behind schedule and straining budgets. A trillion-dollar U.S. play would rival the kingdom’s PIF sovereign wealth fund, which manages about $930 billion.

Yes, this week’s visit delivered real deals: $270 billion in agreements, Nvidia chips for Humain, rare earths partnerships. But the gap between ambition and execution remains vast.

CHART OF THE WEEK

“The Saudi government has accelerated labour market reforms and initiatives to boost the proportion of citizens in the private sector, increasing competition and the pool of job applicants.

Unemployment among Saudi citizens is at a historic low and the number of Saudis in the private sector grew 31% between 2016 and the second quarter of this year.

‘Packages are now far more measured, anchored to data, performance, and real market benchmarks. For some, that feels like contraction. For me, it signals maturity,’ said Louise Knutsson, CEO of Matches Talent in Dubai.” Read Reuters’ full report by Rachna Uppal and Federico Maccioni on how Saudi Arabia is scaling back salary premiums for foreign talent.

THE LAST WAVE: COMAC’s Big Dubai Fly-By, Still No Buyers

China’s state-owned jetmaker COMAC showed up at the Dubai Airshow with its C919 dipping low in a flashy fly-by, belly logo gleaming  for the crowd.

“Where a miracle meets another,” read a poster. The miracle COMAC may be waiting for is that someone outside East Asia actually buys one.

Seventeen years after its debut, the C919 is still waiting for a break. Western certifications? Nope. Global customers? Unless you include orders from Brunei’s startup GallopAir, not really. Yet COMAC pitched a stretched variant to rival Airbus and Boeing’s hottest models—because if you’re going to dream big, do it in Dubai.

Inside the halls where deals happen, the buzz was about Boeing and Airbus orders, not bets on a plane that relies on 18 Western suppliers and has missed its own targets.

data.title: Waymo Prepares to Launch Robotaxis in Three New Cities
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Waymo is gearing up to launch its autonomous ride-hailing services in three new cities.

The self-driving car company, owned by Google parent Alphabet, said Thursday it is laying the groundwork to begin operations in Minneapolis, New Orleans and Tampa, Fla.

In preparation for Minneapolis' winter weather, the company said it has been testing its operations in heavier snow, leveraging artificial intelligence and robust cleaning mechanisms to sustain fully autonomous operations in snow and ice.

Waymo additionally noted that its self-driving cars are adapting to new cities faster as the company begins operating in more markets, helping set up its launch in New Orleans and Tampa.

The company, whose autonomous taxi services are already available in cities including Los Angeles, Phoenix and San Francisco, has been quickly expanding its footprint. Waymo said earlier this year that it planned to begin testing its self-driving cars in more than 10 new cities, including New York City and San Diego.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Fitch Affirms KBC Group NV at 'A'; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Warsaw-20 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has affirmed KBC Group NV's and KBC Bank NV's Long-Term Issuer Default Ratings (IDR) at 'A' and 'A+', respectively. The Outlooks are Stable. Fitch has also affirmed KBC's and KBC Bank's Viability Ratings (VRs) at 'a'. A full list of rating actions is below.

Key Rating Drivers

Strong Business Profile: KBC's ratings reflect a strong, diversified and stable profitability and funding profile, underpinned by leading franchises in banking and insurance in Belgium and the Czech Republic. We consider KBC's strong internal capital generation and adequate risk controls in less developed and more volatile markets in central and eastern Europe. Asset quality is a rating weakness, but we expect it to remain resilient with contained credit losses.

Ratings Reflect Consolidated Group: The failure risk of KBC and its main subsidiary KBC Bank (90% of group assets) is substantially the same, given the high integration of insurance and banking operations. The former is the group's holding company and its VR is equalised with KBC Bank's. The group is regulated on a consolidated basis and holding company double leverage is maintained at below 120%. Liquidity is managed centrally and capital fungibility is high.

Moderate Risk Appetite: The group's conservative risk appetite is supported by the dominance of fairly low-risk and stable operations in Belgium and the Czech Republic. Risks in exposure to the more volatile markets of Slovakia, Bulgaria and Hungary are mitigated by strong integration of risk controls and credit standards within the group. Prudent management of insurance risks leads to low risk of losses.

Stable Asset Quality: KBC's loans are mainly concentrated in Belgium and the Czech Republic with a record of sound impaired loans ratio and loan impairment charges, which we expect to continue. Asset quality in Slovakia, Hungary and Bulgaria should also remain under control due to strong underwriting, although these markets are more sensitive to credit cycle swings. We forecast the consolidated impaired loans ratio to remain below 2% in 2026, while loan impairment charges should be contained at about 20bp of loans.

Robust Earnings: KBC's profitability is a rating strength, underpinned by resilient earnings, even in a low-interest-rate environment. We expect the bank to remain one of the most profitable in the eurozone. Its results benefit from sound business and geographic diversification, low reliance on net interest income, an integrated bancassurance business, sound pricing power and good cost efficiency. We expect a robust operating profit/risk-weighted assets ratio of 3.4% in 2025 and a small increase in the following years, fueled by growth in loans, insurance products and assets under management.

Strong Internal Capital Generation: KBC's net profit/risk-weighted assets ratio averaged about 2.5% over the past decade, including a trough in 2020 at 1.4%. We also consider in our capitalisation assessment the bank's stable asset quality and good geographic diversification between emerging and developed markets. KBC has a minimum acceptable unfloored fully loaded common equity Tier 1 (CET1) ratio at 13% following its updated capital policy in May 2025. We expect the ratio to remain close to 14.5% in 2026 and 2027. A material reduction would require either a sizeable acquisition or a discretionary extraordinary capital distribution, which we do not expect.

Sound Funding and Liquidity: KBC's funding and liquidity profile is a rating strength, underpinned by a diversified retail and SME deposit franchise that is its main source of funding. All subsidiaries, except for Slovakia, are self-funded. We expect KBC's consolidated loans/deposit ratio to modestly exceed 90% at end-2025 and remain close to this level in the next years. KBC has good access to wholesale funding markets, upcoming maturities are reasonably balanced, and an ample liquidity buffer adequately mitigates refinancing risk.

Rating Sensitivities Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

KBC's and KBC Bank's ratings are mainly sensitive to a substantial and durable deterioration in asset quality, translating into erosion of the bank's profitability and capital generation. This could be signaled by a increase in the impaired loans ratio to above 3%, operating profit durably below 3% of risk-weighted assets and our expectation of the group's CET1 ratio falling below 14% without prospects of a swift recovery.

An adverse change in KBC's risk appetite could be negative for the ratings, for example, if the contribution of more volatile central and eastern Europe countries to KBC's earnings rise rapidly, as this could imply an increase in exposure to weaker operating environments.

KBC's ratings could also be downgraded should the holding company's double leverage rise above 120% on a sustained basis without a clear path to reduction, although this is not Fitch's expectation.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

Positive changes for KBC's and KBC Bank's ratings would require a stronger operating environment assessment for the group, accompanied by a stronger business and risk profile leading to improved asset quality while maintaining strong profitability.

OTHER DEBT AND ISSUER RATINGS: KEY RATING DRIVERS

KBC Group and KBC Bank

KBC Bank's Long-Term IDR and long-term senior unsecured debt rating of 'A+' are one notch above the bank's VR, because the risk of default on senior obligations, as measured by the Long-Term IDR, is lower than the risk of the bank failing, as measured by its VR.

The group adopts a single-point-of-entry resolution strategy with bail-in as the preferred resolution tool. KBC is the resolution entity, which meets the group's minimum requirements for own funds and eligible liabilities. Minimum requirements for own funds and eligible liabilities instruments issued by KBC are downstreamed to KBC Bank as junior-ranking instruments to third-party senior debt. For the same reason, KBC's long-term senior unsecured debt is rated 'A'.

KBC's and KBC Bank's Short-Term IDR and short-term senior unsecured debt rating of 'F1' is the lower of two options that map to their respective long-term ratings, reflecting our 'a+' assessment of funding and liquidity.

The bank's Derivative Counterparty Rating (DCR) is aligned with its Long-Term IDR because, under Belgian legislation, derivative counterparties have no preferential status over other senior obligations in a resolution.

The subordinated Tier 2 debt securities issued by KBC are notched down twice from its VR, reflecting baseline notching for loss severity for this type of debt under Fitch's criteria.

KBC's additional Tier 1 (AT1) notes are rated four notches below its VR, reflecting loss severity (two notches) and their high risk of non-performance (two notches) due to fully discretionary coupons. We expect KBC to operate with a CET1 ratio comfortably above maximum distributable amount thresholds.

KBC IFIMA S.A.

KBC IFIMA S.A.'s senior debt rating is aligned with KBC Bank's IDRs, because all debt obligations of KBC IFIMA are unconditionally and irrevocably guaranteed by KBC Bank.

Government Support Rating

KBC's and KBC Bank's Government Support Ratings of 'no support' reflect Fitch's view that sovereign support for senior creditors of KBC and KBC Bank cannot be relied on given the implementation of the Bank Resolution and Recovery Directive in Belgium.

OTHER DEBT AND ISSUER RATINGS: RATING SENSITIVITIES

KBC Group and KBC Bank

KBC's and KBC Bank's Long-Term IDR, DCR and long-term senior unsecured debt ratings are sensitive to changes in KBC's VR. In addition, the bank's ratings could be downgraded to the same level as KBC's if the resolution buffers channeled into the bank no longer provide protection to creditors in a bank failure.

KBC's and KBC Bank's Short-Term IDRs and short-term senior unsecured debt ratings are sensitive to their corresponding long-term ratings and to the funding and liquidity score.

KBC Bank's DCR could also be upgraded in case of a change in legislation giving preference to derivative counterparties over senior obligation in a resolution.

The ratings of KBC's subordinated Tier 2 debt and AT1 notes are sensitive to changes in the VR. The ratings of AT1 notes could be downgraded if the non-performance risk relative to the risk captured in KBC's VR increases, for instance if capital buffers over coupon emission triggers fall below 100bp.

KBC IFIMA S.A.

The senior debt ratings issued by KBC IFIMA are sensitive to a change in KBC Bank's IDRs.

Government Support Rating

An upgrade of KBC's and KBC Bank's Government Support Ratings would be contingent on a positive change in the Belgian sovereign's propensity to support its banks. While not impossible, this is highly unlikely.

VR ADJUSTMENTS

The operating environment score of 'a' is below the 'aa' implied category score and below the 'a+' score of domestic Belgian banks due to the following adjustment reasons: sovereign rating (negative) and geographical scope (negative).

The asset quality score of 'a-' is above the 'bbb' implied category score due to the following adjustment reason: impaired loan formation (positive).

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. Public Ratings with Credit Linkage to other ratings

The ratings of the senior debt issued by KBC IFIMA are driven by the ratings of KBC Bank, which guarantees the notes.

ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

data.title: Florida insurer American Integrity slumps on secondary offering by top holder, CEO
data.description:
** American Integrity Insurance's shares down 5.8% at $19.56 early Thurs after $60 mln secondary offering priced

** AII shares falling for fourth straight session and tracking ~21% weekly loss

** Tampa, Florida-based residential property insurer late Weds said existing holders parted ways with 3 mln shares at $20, a 3.7% discount to last close

** Co launched the offering late Mon

** Real estate investor James Sowell offloaded ~2.7 mln shares, cutting his stake to 24.4%, and CEO Robert Ritchie sold 550,578 shares, per the final offering prospectus

** AII, which has ~19.6 mln shares outstanding, went public on May 8 after ~6.9-mln-share IPO priced at $16

** KBW, Piper Sandler, and William Blair are active joint bookrunners for the secondary offering, joined by passive bookrunners Citizens Capital Markets and Raymond James

** 5 of 6 analysts covering AII rate stock "strong buy" or "buy", 1 has "hold", their median PT is $27.50, per LSEG

data.title: Buyout shops will resume eating the software world
data.description:
By Pranav Kiran

Financial data manager OneStream, worth some $5 bln, is mulling a sale 18 months after its IPO. It’s not alone enduring a share slump as AI hype distracts investors from subscription-based cash flow. For private equity firms with ample funding options, these are easy pickings.

Full view will be published shortly.

CONTEXT NEWS

Financial software developer OneStream is exploring strategic options, including a possible sale, Reuters reported on November 5 citing unnamed sources.

OneStream has been working with investment bankers at JPMorgan, according to the report, which noted that deliberations are at an early stage and that there is no guarantee of any transaction. Buyout firms Blackstone and Hg are among those studying possible bids.

data.title: FCA Chain (FCA) Is Now Available for Trading at LBank Exchange
data.description:
Road Town, British Virgin Islands--(Newsfile Corp. - November 20, 2025) - LBank Exchange, a leading global cryptocurrency trading platform, listed FCA Chain (FCA) at 06:00 on November 19, 2025 (UTC). Users are able to access the FCA/USDT trading pair at: https://www.lbank.com/trade/fca_usdt.

FCA listing banner

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/8831/275285_401f594754a06b91_001full.jpg

About FCA Chain

FCA Chain is an AI-powered privacy computing public blockchain designed to solve some of the most pressing challenges in modern Web3 infrastructure, including data privacy, energy efficiency, AI integration, and cross-chain interoperability. It aims to become the foundational infrastructure for the next generation of decentralized applications by ensuring that sensitive personal and enterprise data—such as photos, documents, contracts, and media—is encrypted, desensitized, and processed securely on-chain. This design preserves user privacy while enabling advanced AI capabilities.

The project integrates AI across the core blockchain layer, optimizing resource allocation, data processing, and governance. FCA Chain allows both high-performance validator nodes and lightweight edge devices such as IoT hardware to participate in network construction, improving inclusivity and sustainability. Through IBC cross-chain communication, EVM/WASM compatibility, decentralized storage services, and AI-assisted governance, FCA Chain provides a flexible and intelligent environment for developers, enterprises, and users.

Core Features

FCA Chain introduces a privacy computing infrastructure where all on-chain data undergoes encryption and sharded desensitization. Only the data owner can decrypt their information, and AI training is performed on non-sensitive feature codes rather than raw data.

It achieves full AI integration by embedding AI into the underlying network, enabling intelligent resource management, automated optimization, and accessible AI capabilities for ordinary users and devices.

The chain supports broad device inclusivity through a high-performance PoS consensus and light-client architecture that welcomes IoT devices, mobile hardware, and consumer terminals into network staking and distributed storage.

Cross-chain functionality is enabled through the IBC protocol, allowing asset and data interoperability with ecosystems such as Ethereum, BNB Chain, Polkadot, and Cosmos, while NAS and CDN services support decentralized storage and content delivery.

The blockchain architecture supports EVM and WASM smart contracts, enabling developers to deploy DeFi, NFT, AI, and modular applications with flexibility and high performance.

Tokenomics

FCA Chain's native token, FCA, has a total supply of 1,000,000,000 FCA.

For the EU as a whole, consumer sentiment edged down 0.1 points to -13.6.

data.title: Eurozone Consumer Sentiment Flat in November
data.description:
By Don Nico Forbes

Eurozone consumer confidence remained flat in November despite cooling inflation and recent economic resilience against trade turbulence, a monthly indicator showed Thursday.

The European Commission's flash consumer-confidence indicator for the eurozone was steady at minus 14.2, in line with October. A consensus of economists polled by The Wall Street Journal expected minus 14.0.

"Consumer confidence remained below its long-term average," the EC said.

The figures come ahead of a reading of business activity across the eurozone, due Friday, which is expected to have picked up in November. Activity has so far proved resilient to trade volatility over the course of 2025, while falling inflation might have provided a further boost to sentiment.

On Monday, the Commission increased its expectations for economic growth in the eurozone this year on resilient activity, but said higher-than-forecast tariffs would likely weigh on the bloc in 2026.

Write to Don Nico Forbes at don.forbes@wsj.com

data.title: Cathie Wood Buys Dip in Circle Internet and Bullish. The Crypto Stocks Could Use Some Love. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Nate Wolf

Bullish and Circle Internet Group were two of the hottest initial public offerings of 2025, but the luster — not to mention the share prices — has faded in the months since. Cathie Wood's ARK Invest is buying the dip.

ARK on Wednesday purchased more than 460,000 shares of Bullish, worth around $16.9 million at the close, and more than 215,000 shares of Circle, roughly $15.1 million, according to the investment manager's daily trading file. The purchases were spread across ARK's Innovation, Fintech Innovation, and Next Generation Internet exchange-traded funds.

The two stocks could use some love. Circle surged out of the gates after its June 5 IPO, rising from an offering price of $31 to a closing high of $263.45 on June 23. It has been tougher sledding for the stablecoin issuer since then. Circle hit a record closing low of $69.72 yesterday, down 74% from its peak.

Bullish, meanwhile, climbed more than 80% on its trading debut in August, but has fallen sharply ever since. Shares of the crypto exchange closed Wednesday at an all-time low $36.56, below their $37 offering price.

Bullish stock was up 0.5% in premarket trading Thursday, while Circle rose 1.1%.

ARK, which focuses on high-potential growth stocks, seems to believe these monthslong slides are temporary and make the two companies more attractive bets than they were at their peaks. Wood's firm bought more than 105,000 shares of Bullish last week and has been steadily adding to its positions in both Bullish and Circle to start this week.

Write to Nate Wolf at nate.wolf@barrons.com
